name: stripe_tier
hypothesis: A Stripe-style page rhythm — alternating dark/vault sections, an animated horizontal lifecycle diagram with packets traveling along edges, a customer-style trust strip with per-logo outcome lines, and a correctly-sized sticky-scroll narrative — will register as 'senior craft' and score materially higher on designJudgement than the current home (3/10).
mechanisms: 8-section alternating rhythm: dark hero → vault trust strip → dark metrics → dark animated lifecycle → vault sticky-scroll → vault products → dark code+stream → dark CTA
Centered hero with no eyebrow (avoids duplicate-eyebrow critique), dual CTA, video bg

Vault trust strip — 8 framework partners (DSPy, Mastra, LangChain, LlamaIndex, CrewAI, OpenAI, Anthropic, Coinbase) with per-cell outcome line; replaces missing customer logos but follows Stripe's '6-8 logos with named outcomes' pattern

Animated lifecycle diagram — full-width horizontal SVG flow, 6 nodes (Input → Spawn → Route → Run → Snapshot → Bill), 3 packets traveling along the path via animateMotion (offset by 2.3s and 4.6s so two are always in flight; the green packet rides behind to imply 'settled'), reduced-motion hides packets
StoryScroller reused with 3 stages (breaks the 4-count pattern Drew killed at R19) and the 320vh track collapsed to 150vh via CSS override (1.5x — fixes the 4x dead-space critique)
Vault recolor of StoryScroller via :global() overrides — eyebrow/title/lede/stage/panel all flipped to vault tokens with AA-safe contrast (dim purple → indigo, dim grey → secondary-vault)
Three-product BentoCell vault grid with restrained icons + CTA arrows (carries forward AA-safe brand-dim icon color)
hl-build code-on-left + cascading-stream-on-right preserved inline; result annotations match Stripe pattern (→ 0.6s · 0 errors · billed $0.04)
All real numbers throughout: 0.6s, 671 models / 14 providers, 90% WebBench-50, $0.06/vCPU-hr, SOC 2 55/57, 60+ ecosystem partners, $0.04 settled
Subtle gradient accents on key text moments (linear-gradient(135deg, #4F46E5, #818CF8)) — not flat brand color
Dual final CTA (primary + secondary docs link) — Stripe pattern of always offering an escape hatch to the technical buyer
